title: Add chapter and scene navigator with drag-and-drop ordering
description: ## Problem Writers need structure while drafting, but the current editor is a single undifferentiated text area. Without chapters/scenes, users cannot plan pacing, reorder narrative beats, or navigate long manuscripts efficiently. ## Target user Novelists and serial fiction writers managing multi-chapter drafts who need scene-level organization similar to Living Writer clarity, without extra clutter. ## Outcome / KPI - Increase average number of scenes created per active manuscript - Improve navigation efficiency (time to jump to target scene) - Reduce manuscript abandonment after chapter 1 ## Scope - Add left-side manuscript tree: chapters with nested scenes - Support create/edit/delete for chapters and scenes - Drag-and-drop reorder for chapters and scenes (with keyboard-accessible fallback controls) - Editor writes to selected scene, not global document blob - Persist ordering and selection across reloads ## Non-goals - Timeline visualization - Character/worldbuilding ontology - Collaboration features ## Acceptance criteria - User can create chapter + scene and switch between scenes without data loss - Reordering scenes updates persisted order and survives refresh - Scene title updates reflect in navigator and editor header - Deleting a scene requires confirmation and cannot silently remove content - Keyboard users can reorder without mouse-only interactions ## Priority rationale Structured drafting is a core differentiator versus plain document tools and is foundational for future outlining, continuity, and revision intelligence. Shipping this now moves the app from generic editor to story-first product. ## Handoff Assign to: Market Research ## Goal Add a chapter/scene navigator with hierarchical manuscript tree, scene-specific editing, and persistent scene content storage. ## Scope and files **New files (9):** 1. `src/types/chapter.ts` - Chapter type definitions 2. `src/lib/chapters.ts` - Chapter hierarchy CRUD operations 3. `src/components/ManuscriptNavigator/ManuscriptNavigator.tsx` - Main tree navigator 4. `src/components/ManuscriptNavigator/ManuscriptNavigator.module.css` - Navigator styles 5. `src/components/ChapterRow/ChapterRow.tsx` - Collapsible chapter row with nested scenes 6. `src/components/ChapterRow/ChapterRow.module.css` - Chapter row styles 7. `src/components/SceneRow/SceneRow.tsx` - Scene row with drag handle and actions 8. `src/components/SceneRow/SceneRow.module.css` - Scene row styles 9. `src/components/ConfirmDialog/ConfirmDialog.tsx` + `.module.css` - Reusable delete confirmation modal **Modified files (4):** 1. `src/types/scene.ts` - Add `content?: string` field for scene body text 2. `src/lib/scenes.ts` - Add scene CRUD helpers (create, updateContent, updateTitle, delete) 3. `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` - Add navigator, scene selection state, scene-scoped editor 4. `src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css` - Three-column grid layout with responsive collapse ## Step-by-step plan ### Phase 1: Data model and persistence foundation **Step 1.1: Extend Scene type with content field** - File: `src/types/scene.ts` - Add `content?: string` field to Scene interface for scene body text - Add `chapterId?: string` field (optional for v1, supports future normalized structure) - Keep existing fields: id, title, chapter, summary, status, order **Step 1.2: Create Chapter type definitions** - File: `src/types/chapter.ts` (new) - Define `Chapter` interface: `id: string`, `title: string`, `order: number`, `expanded?: boolean` (UI state) - Define `ChapterWithScenes` helper type: `{ chapter: Chapter; scenes: Scene[] }` - Export types for use in components and lib functions **Step 1.3: Add chapter-level operations** - File: `src/lib/chapters.ts` (new) - `getChapterGroups(storyId: string): ChapterWithScenes[]` - Group scenes by chapter string, return hierarchical structure - `createChapter(storyId: string, title: string): void` - Add chapter metadata (no scenes created yet) - `updateChapterTitle(storyId: string, oldTitle: string, newTitle: string): void` - Update chapter field for all scenes in that chapter - `deleteChapter(storyId: string, chapterTitle: string): void` - Remove all scenes with that chapter value - `reorderChapter(storyId: string, chapterTitle: string, newOrder: number): void` - Update order for all scenes in chapter - Storage key: Reuse existing `story-${storyId}-scenes` for scenes, add `story-${storyId}-chapters` for chapter metadata **Step 1.4: Add scene CRUD helpers to scenes lib** - File: `src/lib/scenes.ts` (modify) - `createScene(storyId: string, chapterName: string, title: string): Scene` - Create scene with empty content, auto-increment order - `updateSceneContent(storyId: string, sceneId: string, content: string): void` - Save editor content to localStorage - `updateSceneTitle(storyId: string, sceneId: string, title: string): void` - Rename scene - `deleteScene(storyId: string, sceneId: string): void` - Remove scene from storage - Update existing `getScenes` to include content field (default to empty string for backward compatibility) ### Phase 2: Core navigator components **Step 2.1: Build reusable ConfirmDialog** - Files: `src/components/ConfirmDialog/ConfirmDialog.tsx`, `.module.css` - Props: `open: boolean`, `title: string`, `message: string`, `confirmLabel: string`, `cancelLabel: string`, `onConfirm: () => void`, `onCancel: () => void` - Render: Modal overlay (`position: fixed`, `--color-black-700` with 50% opacity), centered dialog card on white background - Keyboard: Esc to cancel, Enter to confirm, focus trap within dialog - Accessibility: `role="dialog"`, `aria-labelledby` for title, `aria-describedby` for message, restore focus to trigger element on close - Style: Grayscale tokens, two-button footer (Cancel secondary, Confirm primary with darker background) **Step 2.2: Build SceneRow component** - Files: `src/components/SceneRow/SceneRow.tsx`, `.module.css` - Props: `scene: Scene`, `active: boolean`, `onClick: () => void`, `onDelete: () => void`, `onRename: (newTitle: string) => void`, `dragHandleProps?: object` - Render: Drag handle icon (visible on hover/focus, uses `dragHandleProps`), scene title (editable on double-click or F2), optional delete button - States: Default, hover (`--color-fog-200` background), active (left border `--color-carbon-600` + subtle fill), focus (2px outline `--color-carbon-600`) - Keyboard: Enter/Space to select, Delete key to trigger delete (opens confirmation), F2 to enter rename mode, Esc to cancel rename - Accessibility: `role="button"`, clear focus ring, ARIA label for drag handle ("Reorder scene") - Style: Compact row height (36-40px), text truncation for long titles with tooltip, indented from chapter row **Step 2.3: Build ChapterRow component** - Files: `src/components/ChapterRow/ChapterRow.tsx`, `.module.css` - Props: `chapter: Chapter`, `scenes: Scene[]`, `selectedSceneId: string | null`, `expanded: boolean`, `onToggle: () => void`, `onAddScene: () => void`, `onDeleteChapter: () => void`, `onSceneClick: (sceneId: string) => void`, `onSceneDelete: (sceneId: string) => void`, `onSceneReorder: (sceneId: string, newOrder: number) => void` - Render: - Chapter header: Disclosure triangle icon (rotates 90deg when expanded), chapter title, scene count badge, "+ Scene" button (visible on hover) - Nested scene list: SceneRow components wrapped in @dnd-kit `SortableContext` for drag-drop reorder - Empty state: If no scenes and expanded, show inline "+ Add first scene" row - Keyboard: Enter/Space to expand/collapse, arrow down to first nested scene when expanded - Drag-drop: Use @dnd-kit `verticalListSortingStrategy`, handle `handleDragEnd` to call `onSceneReorder` - Style: Chapter header with `--color-polar-100` background, slightly larger font, scene rows indented 24px **Step 2.4: Build ManuscriptNavigator component** - Files: `src/components/ManuscriptNavigator/ManuscriptNavigator.tsx`, `.module.css` - Props: `storyId: string`, `chapters: ChapterWithScenes[]`, `selectedSceneId: string | null`, `onSceneSelect: (sceneId: string) => void`, `onCreateChapter: () => void`, `onCreateScene: (chapterTitle: string) => void`, `onDeleteChapter: (chapterTitle: string) => void`, `onDeleteScene: (sceneId: string) => void`, `onReorderScene: (sceneId: string, newOrder: number) => void`, `isMobile?: boolean` - Render: - Header: "+ Chapter" primary button, "Manuscript" title - Scrollable chapter list: Map over chapters, render ChapterRow for each - Empty state: If no chapters, show centered empty card with "Create your first chapter" CTA - State: Track expanded chapter IDs, persist to localStorage with key `story-${storyId}-nav-expanded` - Keyboard: Tab through controls, arrow keys navigate between rows (integrate with focus management) - Accessibility: `role="navigation"`, `aria-label="Manuscript navigator"`, focus remains visible - Style: Fixed width 280px, `--color-polar-100` background, `--color-fashion-300` right border, full viewport height, overflow-y auto - Mobile (when `isMobile` prop true): Render as fixed overlay panel with backdrop, add close button, auto-close on scene select ### Phase 3: Editor integration and scene selection **Step 3.1: Add scene selection state** - File: `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` - Add state: `const [selectedSceneId, setSelectedSceneId] = useState(null)` - Add state: `const [chapters, setChapters] = useState([])` - Derive `selectedScene` from scenes array: `const selectedScene = scenes.find(s => s.id === selectedSceneId)` - On mount: Load chapters with `getChapterGroups(id)`, default `selectedSceneId` to first scene or null - Add `handleSceneSelect(sceneId: string)` callback to update selected scene **Step 3.2: Make editor write to selected scene** - File: `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` - Replace `
